description: Immersive UI Effects — screen-space effects, environmental reactions, audio-reactive HUD, and cinematic visual polish inspired by Burnout, F-Zero, NFS Underground, and Geometry Dash.

userStories.title: Dynamic speed vignette (tunnel vision)
userStories.description: As a player, I want the edges of the screen to darken progressively as I go faster so that I feel tunnel vision pulling me into the road.
acceptanceCriteria: A full-canvas radial gradient overlay is drawn each frame (separate from the existing fuel-warning vignette)
At scrollSpeed < 300 px/s: vignette alpha = 0 (invisible)
At scrollSpeed 300→600 px/s: alpha interpolates from 0 → 0.25 (soft dark edges)
At scrollSpeed 600→800 px/s: alpha interpolates from 0.25 → 0.55 (strong tunnel vision)
Color is dark grey/black (rgba(0,0,0,alpha)) — distinct from the red fuel vignette
Drawn after road and traffic layers, before HUD, so it doesn't darken the UI
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: vignette visibly intensifies as speed increases

userStories.title: Chromatic aberration on impact and nitro
userStories.description: As a player, I want the screen to split into colored ghost images on impact and nitro activation so that intense moments feel physically jarring.
acceptanceCriteria: On collision entry (explosionState transition): chromatic aberration activates for 0.4s
On nitro activation: activates for 0.25s at reduced intensity
Effect: draw the current frame tinted red with offset (+3px, 0) and blue with offset (-3px, 0) at globalAlpha 0.4 using globalCompositeOperation = 'screen', on top of the normal frame
Intensity decays linearly over the duration (from full to 0)
Uses an offscreen canvas to capture the frame before applying the split
Does NOT run during normal gameplay (zero cost when inactive)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: RGB split visible on explosion start and nitro boost

userStories.title: Motion blur / frame ghosting at high speed
userStories.description: As a player, I want fast-moving traffic and the road to leave subtle ghost trails when I'm at top speed so that the game feels like it's on the edge of control.
acceptanceCriteria: An offscreen canvas retains the previous frame
At scrollSpeed > 600 px/s: composite previous frame at globalAlpha 0.18 before drawing current frame
At scrollSpeed > 700 px/s: increase ghost alpha to 0.28
Effect only active above 600 px/s — zero performance cost below that threshold
Ghost is drawn only on the road/traffic layer, not on HUD elements (HUD drawn on top after ghost)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: ghosting trails visible on vehicles at high speed

userStories.title: Bloom / glow on collectibles and nitro exhaust
userStories.description: As a player, I want collectible items and nitro flames to emit light so that they look like they glow and are visually distinct from traffic.
acceptanceCriteria: Collectibles (coin, fuel, nitro, shield) have a colored halo: draw item a second time at 2x scale with filter: blur(8px) and globalCompositeOperation = 'lighter' at globalAlpha 0.5
Nitro exhaust particles already rendered with 'lighter' compositing — boost particle radius by 30% and increase alpha by 0.15 when scrollSpeed > 600
Player car headlights (front two white circles) rendered with blur glow when active
Bloom uses an offscreen canvas: render bloom targets there, then composite onto main canvas
No bloom on traffic vehicles (performance constraint)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: collectibles visibly glow; nitro trail is brighter at high speed

userStories.title: Dynamic FOV — road perspective convergence with speed
userStories.description: As a player, I want the road to appear to narrow and accelerate toward a vanishing point as I go faster so that I feel the perspective crushing in on me.
acceptanceCriteria: The road drawing function uses a fixed trapezoid (left/right edge coordinates) — make the top edge width (near vanishing point) dynamically narrower at high speed
At base speed: road top width = current value (no change)
At scrollSpeed 600 px/s: top width reduces by 15%
At scrollSpeed 800 px/s: top width reduces by 30% (maximum convergence)
Road lane markings and edges interpolate smoothly (no jumps)
Change applies only to the drawn road geometry — player car position and traffic lanes are unaffected
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: road visibly narrows at high speed; traffic/player position unchanged

userStories.title: Camera roll on lateral movement
userStories.description: As a player, I want the canvas to tilt slightly when I swerve left or right so that the car feels like it has physical weight and momentum.
acceptanceCriteria: Track lateral input: left key → target roll = +2.5°, right key → target roll = -2.5°, no key → target roll = 0°
Actual roll eases toward target using spring interpolation: roll += (target - roll) * 0.12 per frame
Apply ctx.rotate(roll * Math.PI / 180) around canvas center before all world rendering
Roll does NOT apply to HUD elements (save/restore ctx around world render, draw HUD after)
Maximum roll capped at ±3° to prevent disorientation
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: visible screen tilt when pressing left/right; HUD stays level

userStories.title: Heat haze / road shimmer at high speed
userStories.description: As a player, I want the road surface to shimmer and distort at high speed so that the game feels like the asphalt is burning up beneath you.
acceptanceCriteria: At scrollSpeed > 500 px/s: animated horizontal wavy distortion applied to the lower 40% of the canvas (road surface area)
Implemented by rendering road to offscreen canvas then drawing back with per-band y-offset: sinOffset = Math.sin(time * 3 + y * 0.08) * intensity
Intensity: 0 below 500 px/s, ramps to max 3px offset at 800 px/s
The distortion applies only to the road layer — traffic and player are drawn normally on top
No shimmer during explosionState or gameOverState
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: road surface wavy distortion visible at max speed

userStories.title: Beat-pulse HUD elements (audio-reactive)
userStories.description: As a player, I want HUD elements to visually pulse in sync with the music beat so that the game feels unified between audio and visual.
acceptanceCriteria: Beat scheduler (already in codebase) fires a beatPulse event or sets a flag lastBeatTime on each kick drum beat
Score counter and speed indicator scale to 1.08x for 80ms then ease back to 1.0x on each beat
Nitro meter glows brighter (alpha +0.3 for 80ms) on each beat when nitro is active
Coin counter bounces scale 1.15x for 60ms when a coin is collected (regardless of beat)
All pulse animations use exponential decay (not linear) for snappy feel
Pulse only active during playingState — no pulse on title/game over screens
Beat sync relies on the AudioContext.currentTime beat scheduler from the musical audio branch — this story adds the visual response hook, not the audio scheduler itself
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: HUD elements visibly pulse with the music beat

userStories.title: Floating pickup text
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see floating text rise from the point where I collect items so that every pickup feels acknowledged and satisfying.
acceptanceCriteria: On coin collect: spawn '+1' (or '+5' for coin clusters) in yellow at the coin's canvas position
On fuel collect: spawn '+FUEL' in green
On nitro collect: spawn '+NITRO' in cyan
On shield collect: spawn '+SHIELD' in blue
On near-miss (existing system): spawn 'NEAR MISS!' in orange
Text floats upward 60px over 0.9s, fading from alpha 1.0 to 0 over that duration
Font: bold 16px, same font family as HUD
Text is drawn on top of all world elements but below the main HUD panel
Max 8 simultaneous floating texts (oldest removed if exceeded)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: text visibly rises from collected items and fades out

userStories.title: Screen-edge danger flash
userStories.description: As a player, I want the screen edges to flash red when a dangerous vehicle is approaching so that I get a visceral off-screen threat warning.
acceptanceCriteria: When any traffic vehicle is within 120px of the player's y-position (near-miss proximity): trigger a red edge flash
Flash: draw a red radial gradient from screen edges inward (rgba(255,0,0,0) center to rgba(255,0,0,0.35) edges) that decays over 0.3s
Multiple simultaneous vehicles can stack flashes (alpha adds, max 0.5 total)
Flash does NOT trigger during explosionState (already in explosion, no need)
Distinct from the existing fuel-warning vignette (this is red with rapid onset, the fuel vignette is slow pulse)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: screen edges flash red when traffic passes close to the player

userStories.title: Hit stop on major collision
userStories.description: As a player, I want the game to freeze for a few frames when I crash so that the collision feels heavy and impactful, not instant.
acceptanceCriteria: On transition to explosionState: freeze the game loop update for exactly 3 frames (approx 50ms at 60fps)
During freeze: update(dt) is not called, but render() continues (screen does not blank)
After freeze: normal explosionState animation proceeds
Hit stop is implemented as a hitStopFrames counter: set to 3 on explosion entry, decremented each frame in gameLoop, updates skipped while > 0
Does NOT affect audio playback (AudioContext continues normally)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: brief freeze visible at moment of explosion before explosion animation plays

userStories.title: Adaptive HUD opacity
userStories.description: As a player, I want the HUD to be subtle during normal play and visible only when values are changing so that the road stays my focus.
acceptanceCriteria: All HUD elements (score, speed, fuel, coins) render at 65% opacity during steady state
When a value changes: that element's opacity ramps to 100% over 100ms, stays for 1.5s, then fades back to 65% over 500ms
Fuel bar always at 100% opacity when below 30% fuel (critical state override)
Implemented per-element with hudOpacity trackers that store {value, lastChanged, currentAlpha}
ctx.globalAlpha is saved/restored around each HUD element draw
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: HUD elements are subdued at steady state, brighten when score/fuel changes
